key_drivers: Broad USD softness remains the dominant cross-asset support even after a small daily DXY rebound.
Middle East supply disruption is keeping crude supported despite the latest bearish U.S. inventory build.
Soft U.S. growth, slightly wider credit spreads, and a flatter 2s10s curve continue to weigh on cyclical U.S. equity segments.
A hawkish RBA, expansionary China PMI, and firm copper keep AUD as the strongest bullish expression in the framework.

stale_sources: World Gold Council ETF flow data published 2026-04-08
Latest fully published EIA weekly oil inventory data still covers the week ended 2026-04-03
ECB, RBA, and BoJ policy assessments rely on the latest official March meeting communications
fallbacks_used: TradingView DXY quote page for current dollar direction
TradingView MOVE quote page for current rates-volatility direction
TradingView SOX quote page for the semiconductor factor
TradingView copper quote page for the industrial-metals factor
